key_facts: `INTEGRATION_REGISTRY` is populated in `src/specify_cli/integrations/__init__.py` by registering built-in `IntegrationBase` subclasses alphabetically.

`MarkdownIntegration`, `TomlIntegration`, and `SkillsIntegration` in `base.py` are the main generation families; they process shared command templates, append runtime project-map gates, append delegation/result contracts for selected workflows, and install integration scripts.

Codex, Claude, Kimi, and Antigravity use skills-style surfaces; Codex also includes `team.md` and `implement-teams.md` and injects Codex-native multi-agent guidance into map/test/spec/plan/task/implement/debug skills.
Copilot is custom: it writes `.agent.md` files, companion `.prompt.md` files, and VS Code settings instead of using the standard Markdown base directly.
Forge is custom: it replaces `$ARGUMENTS` with `{{parameters}}`, strips incompatible `handoffs` frontmatter, and injects a `name` field.
`templates/commands/map-scan.md` and `templates/commands/map-build.md` now define the brownfield two-step scan/build contract with state, packet, evidence, and reverse coverage requirements.
Generated update-context scripts and shared scripts are product code because changes propagate into downstream initialized repositories.
recommended_atlas_updates: Document integration key conventions and the no-shorthand CLI-tool-name rule.
Document the shared template-to-agent propagation chain and special adapter exceptions.
Document map-scan/map-build as generated workflow contracts, not implementation code.
unknowns: Real external CLI behavior for each agent can drift independently of generated adapter assumptions.
